title: [InstCombine][profcheck] Fix profile metadata propagation for umax in InstCombine
author: snehasish
description: Select instructions created from the expansion of an umax intrinsic do not have profile data even though the function may have profile data. This is because PGO instrumentation does not support intrinsics.

tests.test_body:
declare void @use(i64)

define i64 @test_mul_multi_use_mul(i64 %x) {
  %x1 = add i64 %x, 1
  %mul = mul nuw i64 %x, 3
  call void @use(i64 %mul)
  %max = call i64 @llvm.umax.i64(i64 %mul, i64 %x1)
  ret i64 %max
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i64 @llvm.umax.i64(i64, i64) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

comments.author: snehasish
comments.body:
> Why is this not using the usual unknown branch weight metadata?

Thanks for the reminder. I'll make the change - I'm preparing a stack of changes all addressing similar issues in InstCombine. It needs a bit more polish before it's ready for review.
